hero.headline:
Real Estate Advisory
for Diplomatic Missions
hero.subtext: Strategic housing, acquisition, and long-term stewardship in global capitals.
hero.description: We advise diplomatic, foreign and private organizations on real estate decisions, helping balance financial responsibility, operational needs, and long-term presence.

cards.title: Long-Term Lens
cards.text: Diplomatic housing decisions are rarely short-term. We evaluate real estate through a multi-decade horizon, accounting for cost, risk, continuity, and asset value.

cards.title: From Global to Local
cards.text: We work with both headquarters and local missions, ensuring alignment between strategic objectives and on-the-ground realities.

cards.title: Discretion & Precision
cards.text: Our advisory work is deliberate, analytical, and discreet. We prioritize clarity over speed and outcomes over volume.

services.headline: Three Pillars of Advisory
services.subtext: Our work spans the full lifecycle of diplomatic real estate — from strategic planning through acquisition to ongoing stewardship.

about.headline: Confidence in Complexity
paragraphs: Our advisory practice has grown from years working with diplomatic missions, international organizations, and global clients navigating New York's real estate market.

We combine market expertise with an understanding of diplomatic process, institutional decision-making, and long-term stewardship — helping missions operate with confidence in one of the world's most complex cities.

cta.headline: Schedule a Consultation
cta.subtext: We advise quietly, plan across decades, and measure success by stability, continuity, and outcomes over time.

faqs.question: What is diplomatic housing advisory?
faqs.answer: Diplomatic housing advisory is a specialized real estate service that helps diplomatic missions, consulates, and international organizations navigate property acquisition, leasing, and portfolio management in global capitals. It combines market expertise with an understanding of diplomatic process and institutional decision-making.

faqs.question: Why should diplomatic missions consider buying property instead of renting?
faqs.answer: Over a 20–30 year horizon, purchasing property can save a diplomatic mission $45–60 million compared to renting, depending on location, unit count, and market conditions. Ownership builds equity, eliminates exposure to annual rent increases of 3–5%, and provides long-term operational stability for staff housing.

faqs.question: What services does Aabo Advisory provide?
faqs.answer: Aabo Advisory provides three core services: Strategic Housing Advisory (rent vs. buy analysis, portfolio strategy, financial modeling), Transaction & Representation (acquisition, disposals, leasing, negotiation), and Operational Stewardship (post-closing oversight, vendor coordination, ongoing asset monitoring).

faqs.question: Which markets does Aabo Advisory serve?
faqs.answer: Aabo Advisory primarily serves diplomatic missions and international organizations operating in New York City, one of the most complex and high-value real estate markets in the world. Our advisory framework is designed for sovereign and institutional clients navigating this market.

faqs.question: How does the Buy vs. Rent Calculator work?
faqs.answer: Our Buy vs. Rent Calculator models the long-term financial impact of owning versus leasing diplomatic housing. It factors in purchase price, monthly costs, property appreciation, rent escalation, transaction costs, and hold period to produce a multi-decade cost comparison with detailed annual projections.
